LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS ANNA BRADY AND JANELLE MATTHEWS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA COURTS EMPLOY "GREAT LIBERALITY IN ALLOWING AMENDMENTS," PARTICULARLY WHERE THEY "RELATE TO THE SAME GENERAL SET OF FACTS." (DOUGLAS V. SUP. CT. (1989) 215 CAL.APP.3D 155, 158; KITTREDGE SPORTS CO. V. SUP. CT. (1989) 213 CAL.APP.3D 1045, 1048.) IN THE "SYCAMORE CLAIMS," PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT CEO BRIAN "O'CALLAGHAN POOLED PLAINTIFFS' AND OTHER EMPLOYEES' PRINCIPAL CONTRIBUTIONS AND INVESTED ON BEHALF OF A GROUP OF EMPLOYEES." (FAC 9:25-26.) AN ADEQUATE NEXUS EXISTS BETWEEN THESE AND PLAINTIFFS' OTHER CLAIMS REGARDING THEIR EMPLOYMENT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED ND FILED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.

Off Calendar Dec-08-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Michele Gehrke, esq., Ronnie Shou, esq., and Brenda Rosales-Castillo, esq. for Plaintiffs.
Alina Pavlova, esq., Heather Barnes, esq., and Elliot Coz, esq. for Defendants.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFs ANNA BRADY and JANELLE MATTHEWS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions is taken under submission.

Judge Pro Tem: Adrienne Rogers, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Nov-25-2020 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-06-2020 Continued To May-24-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof Declaration Of Michele Haydel Gehrke
Off Calendar Nov-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances by ZOOM Video-conferencing:

Attorney, Michele Haydel Gehrke of the law offices of Reed Smith (Tel: 415.659.4798; Email: mgehrke@reedsmith.com) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Alina Pavlova of the law offices of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney (Tel: 415.788.1900; Email: apavlova@mpbf.com) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

Court having reviewed the pleadings and there being no appearance, tentative ruling is confirmed as follows:

PLAINTIFFs ANNA BRADY and JANELLE MATTHEWS' Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. California courts employ "great liberality in allowing amendments," particularly where they "relate to the same general set of facts." (Douglas v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 155, 158; Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.) In the "Sycamore claims," plaintiffs allege that defendant CEO Brian "O'Callaghan pooled Plaintiffs' and other employees' principal contributions and invested on behalf of a group of employees." (FAC 9:25-26.) An adequate nexus exists between these and plaintiffs' other claims regarding their employment.

Written order is signed nd filed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.
